workflows.name: Earnings call analysis with RAG
workflows.one_liner: Ingest 50-200 transcripts per cycle, run analyst Q&A against them with context stitched from a vector index.
workflows.who_runs_it: Quant and multi-strat hedge funds, long-only research desks. Typically 10-40 analysts per fund running structured Q&A across a full transcript corpus each earnings cycle.
workflows.trigger: Each earnings cycle (4x/year) plus ad-hoc on new prints from covered companies.

cost_breakdown.label: LLM inference (Claude Sonnet 4)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each Q&A round sends a stitched retrieval context plus the analyst question to the model and receives a structured answer with citations.

cost_breakdown.label: System prompt and few-shot examples
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 8,500-token reusable prefix defining output schema, citation format, and analyst persona is prepended to every model call without caching.

cost_breakdown.label: Tool calls and agent framework hops
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each run triggers an extractor, router, and answerer tool chain; each hop is a billed LLM call. LangChain abstracts these into one logical invocation.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Framework abstraction (LangChain / LlamaIndex) hides per-hop billing inside one logical call on the dashboard.

cost_breakdown.label: Vector search and embedding refresh
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each query hits the vector store; new transcript chunks are re-embedded every cycle using text-embedding-3-large at $0.13/M tokens.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Embedding bills land on a separate OpenAI invoice and never join the workflow P&L visible to the CTO.

cost_breakdown.label: Context re-injection across iterations
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Prior Q&A turns are re-sent in full on every follow-up call to preserve thread state. Across 5 calls, accumulated thread tokens total 230k input with no pruning policy active.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Pure overhead - the same tokens are billed again on every turn because no pruning or compression policy has been configured.

workflows.shock_line: A 30-analyst desk spends ~$65k/yr on earnings analysis; 71% is context re-injection and invisible orchestration hops that no one set a policy on.
what_firedog_shows: Per-run cost broken down to the cent across all five components, updated in real time.
Which turn in the Q&A loop billed the most tokens and why history re-injection is the dominant line item.
Exact $/year saved by activating a pruning policy versus routing follow-up turns to Claude Haiku 4.
what_you_can_do: Set a sliding-window pruning policy on history re-injection to cap per-turn context at 10k tokens.
Route follow-up turns to Claude Haiku 4; reserve Sonnet 4 for the first analytical call only.
Cache the system prompt prefix via Anthropic prompt caching to drop that input cost by ~10x.
workflows.meta: Earnings call RAG: 72% of a $65k/yr hedge fund workflow is invisible context re-injection. Firedog instruments it per run.

workflows.name: Market research synthesis (agentic loop)
workflows.one_liner: Multi-source research agent that autonomously browses, retrieves, and synthesizes a structured sector brief.
workflows.who_runs_it: Macro and sector hedge funds, VC research teams, sell-side desk pods. A single brief typically drives 15-40 minutes of wall-clock agentic runtime.
workflows.trigger: Weekly sector updates plus on-demand for event-driven trades; 3-8 briefs per analyst per week.

cost_breakdown.label: LLM inference (Claude Opus 4)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each agentic step sends the current research state plus new retrieved content to Opus 4. Iteration count varies 8-22 steps per brief.

cost_breakdown.label: Research persona and output schema prefix
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 6,000-token system prompt defining research depth, output sections, and citation style is resent on every iteration without prompt caching enabled.

cost_breakdown.label: Planner-executor-verifier loop hops
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each iteration invokes a planner subagent, one or more executor agents, and a verifier; each is a separate billed call. A 14-step brief produces 42 billed hops.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Each planner-executor-verifier trio looks like one 'research step' in logs; the three underlying API calls are never surfaced as separate line items.

cost_breakdown.label: Iterative web and vector retrieval
cost_breakdown.what_happens: The agent fetches and re-embeds sources on each loop iteration. Retrieval is not deduplicated across steps; overlapping sources are re-embedded.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Retrieval cost sits on the vector-DB vendor invoice; no cross-charging to the workflow P&L occurs.

cost_breakdown.label: Full research thread re-sent each iteration
cost_breakdown.what_happens: The complete running research memo is prepended to every iteration call so the agent retains prior reasoning. By step 14 this is 60k+ accumulated tokens sent each time.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Accumulated research state is framed as 'context' in framework logs, not as re-billed tokens, so it does not appear in per-call spend reporting.

workflows.shock_line: One agentic sector brief costs ~$4.90 to run; a 10-analyst fund burns ~$73k/yr and 45% is orchestration overhead and re-billed accumulation no dashboard shows.
what_firedog_shows: Token cost per agentic iteration, showing the inflection point where history accumulation overtakes inference spend.
Which executor agent calls are producing duplicate retrieval and how much that costs per brief.
Projected $/year reduction from capping iteration depth at 10 steps with a summary-compression step.
what_you_can_do: Implement a rolling summary compression after step 5 to prevent unbounded history accumulation.
Deduplicate retrieved sources before re-embedding; roughly 30% of retrieval calls hit already-ingested content.
Route planner and verifier hops to Haiku 4; run only executor calls on Opus 4.
workflows.meta: Agentic sector brief: $4.90/run, 45% invisible. Firedog maps every iteration hop and accumulation spike on a macro fund's research loop.

workflows.name: Regulatory filing review
workflows.one_liner: 10-K, prospectus, and fund-doc review using a long-context model to extract risk factors, covenants, and material changes.
workflows.who_runs_it: Legal, compliance, and investment teams at hedge funds, PE firms, and asset managers. Driven by fund doc review for new allocations and ongoing 10-K monitoring for covered names.
workflows.trigger: SEC filing events (10-K, 10-Q, prospectus, S-1) plus ad-hoc on fund subscription documents for new LP commitments.

scenario.label: PE fund, ongoing portfolio and deal review

cost_breakdown.label: Long-context inference (Gemini 2.5 Pro)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Full document context (90k-200k tokens) is sent in one call. Gemini 2.5 Pro is preferred for the 1M-token flat-rate window and strong extraction on dense legal text.

cost_breakdown.label: Extraction schema and compliance checklist
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 9,500-token prompt encodes the extraction schema (risk categories, covenant types, change-in-control triggers) and is sent twice per run without caching.

cost_breakdown.label: Pre-processing and post-processing pipeline calls
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A chunking agent pre-processes the PDF into structured sections; a validation agent checks extraction completeness. Both are billed model calls.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Pre- and post-processing calls are logged as 'pipeline infra' costs, not attributed to the filing review workflow.

cost_breakdown.label: Precedent-filing vector lookup
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Prior filings from the same issuer are retrieved from the vector store to enable change-detection. Embeddings are regenerated on each run rather than cached.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Embedding regeneration on every run is a design default in LlamaIndex; the cost appears on the embedding-provider invoice, not in the LLM spend view.

cost_breakdown.label: Cross-document comparison context
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Summaries from the prior cycle review are prepended to the current run to enable year-over-year change analysis without storing structured diffs.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Cross-cycle context is framed as 'document memory' and handled by the application layer; it never appears as a token cost line in the model provider bill.

workflows.shock_line: Regulatory filing review costs ~$0.78/run but 59% is re-embedded precedent lookups and cross-cycle context injection hidden across two vendor invoices.
what_firedog_shows: Full per-run attribution split across model, retrieval, and history components with exact token counts.
Which retrieval calls are regenerating embeddings for documents already in the index.
Dollar impact of switching from per-run re-embedding to cached incremental updates.
what_you_can_do: Cache prior-cycle summaries as structured JSON diffs rather than re-injecting full text each run.
Switch to incremental embedding updates so only changed filing sections are re-embedded.
Enable Gemini context caching for the extraction schema prefix to reduce prompt overhead by ~90%.
workflows.meta: 10-K and fund-doc review: $0.78/run, 59% in embedding regeneration and cross-cycle context. Firedog splits the two invoices into one P&L view.

workflows.name: Portfolio monitoring and signal generation
workflows.one_liner: Recurring agentic run across multiple tickers - news, filings, macro data - producing structured trade signals with compounding history.
workflows.who_runs_it: Systematic and quant hedge funds, multi-strat pods, and prop trading desks running intraday or end-of-day signal pipelines across 50-500 names.
workflows.trigger: Scheduled: intraday every 30-60 minutes during market hours, plus event-driven on news alerts and filing filings. History compounds across every run.

cost_breakdown.label: Signal inference (GPT-4.1)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: For each ticker batch, the model receives current news context plus the signal schema and produces a structured JSON signal object.

cost_breakdown.label: Signal schema, ticker metadata, and examples
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 4,200-token prompt encoding signal categories, confidence thresholds, and sector-specific few-shot examples is sent on every ticker-batch call.

cost_breakdown.label: Ticker batching, routing, and aggregation calls
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A router agent groups tickers into batches, dispatches to signal agents, and an aggregator merges outputs. Each produces a separate billed call.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Batching and aggregation calls are attributed to 'orchestration infrastructure' in the platform, not to this workflow's cost center.

cost_breakdown.label: News and filing vector lookups per ticker
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each ticker triggers an independent vector query for the last 24 hours of news and any recent filings. Queries are not batched; 150 tickers produce 150 round trips.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Vector DB query costs are on the Pinecone or Weaviate invoice; no join to the LLM workflow P&L has been built.

cost_breakdown.label: Prior signal state re-injection
cost_breakdown.what_happens: The last 3 signal outputs per ticker are prepended on every run so the model can detect trend changes. Across 150 tickers this accumulates 90k+ tokens per run.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Signal state history is maintained by the application layer as a 'context window' feature; none of the LLM vendor dashboards show it as a distinct cost component.

workflows.shock_line: Portfolio signal generation on 150 tickers costs $0.60/run; run it 12x/day for a year and 61% of the $14.4k annual bill is invisible history re-injection across ticker batches.
what_firedog_shows: Per-run cost with history accumulation graphed over the trading day - showing cost-per-run drift as history grows.
Which tickers are generating the most history volume and driving disproportionate per-batch cost.
Projected annual saving from rolling a 1-signal rather than 3-signal lookback per ticker.
what_you_can_do: Reduce the signal history window from 3 prior runs to 1 per ticker; test accuracy delta before committing.
Batch ticker vector queries into grouped calls; 150 individual round trips is the single largest latency and cost driver.
Route ticker batches with no recent news activity to GPT-5 Mini or Claude Haiku 4 to cut per-run model cost by 75%.
workflows.meta: Portfolio signal generation: 61% of cost is invisible prior-signal history. Firedog tracks accumulation drift per ticker across every intraday run.

workflows.name: Deal and investment memo generation
workflows.one_liner: Structured investment memo from 20-50 source documents - CIM, financials, management decks, diligence notes - with citation-backed sections.
workflows.who_runs_it: PE and VC investment teams, M&A desks at hedge funds. Memo generation is a high-value, low-frequency workflow with high tolerance for cost but zero tolerance for hallucination.
workflows.trigger: New deal entry to IC pipeline; typically 1-4 memos per analyst per month. Revision cycles add 2-5 additional runs per memo.

cost_breakdown.label: Long-form synthesis (Claude Opus 4)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: The model ingests a stitched document context and produces a 4,000-8,000 token structured memo with section-level citations. Opus 4 is used for hallucination risk tolerance reasons.

cost_breakdown.label: IC memo template and section schema
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 12,000-token prompt encodes section headings, financial model hooks, risk taxonomy, and 4 full few-shot examples. Sent on every call without caching.

cost_breakdown.label: Document ingestion and section routing agents
cost_breakdown.what_happens: An ingestion agent chunks and classifies source documents; a routing agent dispatches chunks to section-specific sub-calls; a merge agent assembles the output.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Document ingestion and merge agents are instrumented as 'data pipeline' steps in engineering logs, not as LLM spend in the finance system.

cost_breakdown.label: Source document chunk retrieval
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each section call retrieves the top-12 relevant chunks from the source document index. Re-embedding runs on every revision cycle even when source docs have not changed.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Re-embedding unchanged source docs on revision runs is a known inefficiency; the cost is buried in the embedding vendor line rather than the deal P&L.

cost_breakdown.label: Prior memo draft re-injection on revisions
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Revision runs prepend the full prior memo draft to the model call context so the model can diff its own output. A 5,000-token draft becomes 25,000 tokens re-billed across 5 revisions.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Revision-cycle context is framed as 'draft history' by the application; it appears nowhere in the per-run cost breakdown visible to finance or risk.

workflows.shock_line: An investment memo costs ~$4.80 to generate; revision cycles mean 52% of the $21.5k/yr total is prior-draft context re-injected on every edit nobody is tracking.
what_firedog_shows: Per-run cost by memo section, showing which revision run triggered the largest context spike.
How much of each run's token spend is the prior-draft re-injection versus new synthesis.
Dollar cost of enabling prompt caching on the IC memo template across 250 working days.
what_you_can_do: Store prior drafts as structured section diffs rather than full-text re-injection to cap revision overhead.
Cache the 12,000-token IC memo template prefix; at 3 calls/run and $15/M input this saves roughly $0.54 per run.
Flag revision runs for incremental embedding updates; re-embedding unchanged source docs on every revision is the largest single waste line.
workflows.meta: Investment memo generation: $4.80/run, 52% hidden in revision-cycle draft re-injection. Firedog tracks cost per memo revision across PE deal workflows.

workflows.name: News and filing alert triage
workflows.one_liner: High-frequency classification and summarization of news and SEC filings into structured alerts routed by materiality tier.
workflows.who_runs_it: Trading desks, event-driven hedge funds, and risk teams at large asset managers. Volume is high; latency requirements are strict (sub-30s); models must be fast and cheap.
workflows.trigger: Continuous stream during market hours: ~200-800 raw items per hour depending on market conditions and covered-name universe.

cost_breakdown.label: Classification and summarization (Claude Haiku 4)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each news item or filing alert is sent to Haiku 4 for materiality classification (5-tier) and a 150-token structured summary.

cost_breakdown.label: Classification schema and entity metadata
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 4,800-token prompt carries the materiality taxonomy, covered-name list, and 6 few-shot examples. Sent on every call with no caching enabled.

cost_breakdown.label: Ingestion, dedup, and routing pipeline
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A dedup agent checks the item against a 24-hour seen-items cache; a routing agent sends Tier-1 alerts to a downstream escalation model. Both are billed calls.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Dedup and routing agents are considered 'infrastructure' and billed to the platform cost center, not the signal workflow budget.

cost_breakdown.label: Entity resolution and prior-alert lookup
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each item triggers an entity resolution query to map freetext company names to internal tickers, plus a prior-alert lookup to detect duplicates.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Entity resolution queries are served by an in-house NER model whose inference cost is allocated to the infra team, not the trading workflow.

cost_breakdown.label: Coverage context re-injection
cost_breakdown.what_happens: For Tier-1 items, the last 3 alerts on the same ticker are prepended to provide trend context. This multiplies input tokens for the highest-value subset of items.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Coverage context is injected by the alert-enrichment service; it shows up as increased prompt length in the model bill but is never attributed to this workflow.

workflows.shock_line: At $0.019/run and 400 runs/day, news triage looks trivially cheap - until you realize 57% is uncached prompt overhead, routing infrastructure, and coverage context that scales with alert volume, not analyst headcount.
what_firedog_shows: Total daily and monthly triage cost with per-run breakdown, updated in real time.
Cost-per-alert by materiality tier, showing that Tier-1 items (context-enriched) cost 3x Tier-4 items.
Projected annual saving from caching the classification schema prefix across 400 daily runs.
what_you_can_do: Cache the 3,800-token classification schema prefix; at 400 runs/day and $1/M input, caching saves ~$0.49/day - $122/yr at 90% cache hit rate.
Limit prior-alert context injection to Tier-1 and Tier-2 items only; Tier-3 and below do not benefit from trend context.
Batch Tier-4 and Tier-5 items with a 60-second delay window to reduce per-item call overhead.
workflows.meta: News alert triage: 400 runs/day, 36% hidden in uncached prompt overhead. Firedog tracks cost-per-tier and accumulation drift on high-frequency streams.

workflows.name: Counterparty and KYC document review
workflows.one_liner: Bulk ingestion of counterparty packs, ownership structures, and AML docs with redundant retrieval patterns typical of compliance workflows.
workflows.who_runs_it: Prime brokerage, credit trading, and compliance teams at hedge funds and asset managers. Triggered by new counterparty onboarding, annual re-certification, and credit line reviews.
workflows.trigger: New counterparty onboarding (sporadic, high-doc volume), annual KYC refresh cycles, and credit event reviews. Compliance teams run 5-20 reviews per week.

scenario.label: Hedge fund compliance team, weekly cadence

cost_breakdown.label: Extraction and risk scoring (GPT-4.1)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each document batch is sent to GPT-4.1 for structured entity extraction (beneficial owners, jurisdictions, sanctions flags) and a risk-tier score.

cost_breakdown.label: Compliance checklist and jurisdiction rules
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 9,000-token prompt encodes FATF categories, sanctions list references, and ownership threshold rules. Sent per call without caching.

cost_breakdown.label: Document classification and ownership graph resolution
cost_breakdown.what_happens: An OCR/classification agent pre-processes each document; a graph-resolution agent traces ownership chains. Both produce billed model calls that no workflow monitor attributes to this job.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Ownership graph resolution is run by a separate compliance microservice; its LLM calls land on a different cost center and never appear in the KYC workflow P&L.

cost_breakdown.label: Sanctions database and precedent-case lookups
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each entity triggers an embedding lookup against the sanctions database and a precedent-case vector store. Lookups are not cached between documents in the same counterparty pack.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Sanctions lookup embeddings are managed by the compliance platform vendor; costs appear on the compliance SaaS invoice, not the AI workflow budget.

cost_breakdown.label: Prior review and exception history re-injection
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Prior KYC review summaries for the counterparty are prepended to every call to enable change detection. Annual refresh runs accumulate 2-3 prior review summaries in context.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Prior review summaries are injected by the case-management system as 'counterparty history'; the resulting token cost is not tracked against the AI workflow budget by any current tooling.

workflows.shock_line: KYC document review costs $0.75/run but 66% is split across three separate vendor invoices - sanctions lookups, ownership graph calls, and prior-review history that no single dashboard joins.
what_firedog_shows: Unified per-run view joining LLM, embedding, and compliance-platform costs for the first time.
Which counterparty documents are triggering redundant sanctions lookups due to entity overlap within the same pack.
Dollar impact of caching sanctions lookup results for 24 hours given the low rate of sanctions list updates.
what_you_can_do: Cache sanctions lookup results per named entity for a 24-hour TTL; sanctions lists update daily at most, not per-document.
Deduplicate entity extraction across documents in the same counterparty pack before triggering ownership graph resolution.
Store prior review summaries as structured change-detection objects rather than full text to cap history re-injection at 5,000 tokens per counterparty.
workflows.meta: KYC review: $0.75/run, 66% invisible across three vendor invoices. Firedog is the first tool to join compliance platform, embedding, and LLM costs per review run.

workflows.name: Internal research chatbot over fund knowledge base
workflows.one_liner: Analyst chat interface over a RAG index of internal research notes, models, and meeting transcripts with multi-turn conversation history.
workflows.who_runs_it: Research analysts and portfolio managers at hedge funds and asset managers. Usage is continuous and conversational; session depth compounds cost non-linearly.
workflows.trigger: Ad-hoc analyst queries throughout the trading day. Average session length 6-14 turns. Power users run 8-15 sessions per day.

cost_breakdown.label: Conversational inference (Claude Sonnet 4)
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each analyst turn sends the retrieval context plus the conversation history to Sonnet 4 and gets a grounded answer with source references.

cost_breakdown.label: Analyst persona and source citation rules
cost_breakdown.what_happens: A 5,500-token prompt defining fund-specific terminology, source ranking rules, and hedging language is resent on every turn without caching.

cost_breakdown.label: Query routing and intent classification
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each turn is first sent to a lightweight classifier to determine retrieval strategy (semantic vs. metadata filter vs. hybrid). The classifier call is billed separately.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Intent classification calls are made by the chatbot middleware layer; they land on a platform cost center separate from the analytics workflow budget.

cost_breakdown.label: Knowledge base vector queries per turn
cost_breakdown.what_happens: Each turn retrieves the top-8 chunks from the internal research index. The index is rebuilt nightly including unchanged documents.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Nightly index rebuild re-embeds the full knowledge base regardless of which documents have changed; this cost appears on the embedding-provider invoice, not the chatbot budget.

cost_breakdown.label: Full session history re-sent on every turn
cost_breakdown.what_happens: The complete conversation transcript from turn 1 is resent on every subsequent turn. By turn 8 of a session this is 48k+ tokens of prior dialogue injected as context.
cost_breakdown.invisible_reason: Session history is managed by the chat framework as a first-class feature; the token volume it generates is never disaggregated from the per-turn inference cost in any dashboard the fund currently runs.

workflows.shock_line: The internal research chatbot costs ~$39.5k/yr to run; 47% of that is full session history re-billed on every turn - a cost that compounds with session depth and is invisible in every current dashboard.
what_firedog_shows: Per-session cost curve showing how cost-per-turn grows as session depth increases.
Which analysts are running the deepest sessions and what compounded history cost looks like across their daily usage.
Projected annual saving from implementing a sliding-window summary compression after turn 4.
what_you_can_do: Compress session history into a rolling 2,000-token summary after turn 4; test answer quality delta across 50 representative sessions before rolling out.
Cache the 5,500-token analyst persona prefix via Anthropic prompt caching; at 8 calls/run it saves roughly $0.13/run.
Switch nightly index rebuild to incremental updates; only documents modified in the last 24 hours need re-embedding.
workflows.meta: Internal research chatbot: $39.5k/yr, 47% from session history compounding per turn. Firedog surfaces per-session cost curves analysts and CTOs have never seen before.
